title: SEATTLE FISHERMEN'S MEMORIAL
content: # SEATTLE FISHERMEN'S MEMORIAL. ## The Memorial. In 1988, the Seattle Fishermen's Memorial dedicated this magnificent bronze and stone aggregate monument at Seattle's Fishermen's Terminal. This towering sculpture and the bronze name plaques at its base have become a place of reverence, recognition and healing for the families of more than 675 local commercial fishermen and women who have lost their lives pursuing their livelihood since the turn of the century. More than a tribute to an industry and the lives of its men and women, the monument fills a special need for commercial fishing families. The construction of this monument has provided their memory a residence, a site for family and friends to visit, to place flowers, to reflect and to heal. The Festival celebrates the return of the North Pacific fishing fleet to the terminal, works to increase the public's knowledge of the importance of the fishing industry. Seattle Fishermen's Memorial is dependent upon the generosity of our donors.
